ap5_dependency_refinement.base_susceptibility_update: AP4 is required when route-conditioned selection participates; AP5 is not required for proxy-free susceptibility update
ap5_dependency_refinement.i2_relation: I2 required AP5 for the direct proxy-collapse primitive and froze the general rule that any primitive depending on proxy derivation or target formation must carry AP5. I5 makes that general rule explicit for susceptibility_update instead of treating all susceptibility updates as AP5-dependent.
ap5_dependency_refinement.proxy_conditioned_susceptibility_update: AP5 is required when proxy derivation or target formation participates in susceptibility update

checks.detail: basin signature, drift, floors, boundary, flux, replay, failures, blockers
floors, boundary, flux, and replay present in every row
primitive-specific controls are non-empty per row
controls reject or block claim when triggered
proxy-only success cannot satisfy same-basin continuation
label-only continuation cannot satisfy same-basin continuation
hidden producer support cannot satisfy same-basin continuation

detail.base_susceptibility_update: AP4 is required when route-conditioned selection participates; AP5 is not required for proxy-free susceptibility update
detail.i2_relation: I2 required AP5 for the direct proxy-collapse primitive and froze the general rule that any primitive depending on proxy derivation or target formation must carry AP5. I5 makes that general rule explicit for susceptibility_update instead of treating all susceptibility updates as AP5-dependent.
detail.proxy_conditioned_susceptibility_update: AP5 is required when proxy derivation or target formation participates in susceptibility update

detail.claim_boundary: medium debt is a placeholder for later graph-substrate and agentic-ecology work, not ant-ecology implementation in N20

checks.detail: N21-N28 cannot redefine basin signature, continuation condition, proxy-only success, or producer-residue classification ad hoc in order to pass. A later experiment may only revise an I5 definition by recording source-backed evidence that the definition is over- or under-constraining, while preserving N19 AP4/AP5 gap propagation and unsafe claim blockers.

claim_boundary: N20 Iteration 5 completes contract rows only. It does not test or support withdrawal resistance, naturalization depth, susceptibility update, live-continuation collapse, optionality, spark/new-basin formation, proxy collapse, transfer, generative persistence, agency, Phase 8, native support, sentience, ant ecology, or semantic function.

definition_component_validation.downstream_revision_policy: N21-N28 may refine the field list only by recording source-backed geometry showing that the declared signature is over- or under-specific
definition_component_validation.risk_if_omitted: label, proxy, or output could replace the basin itself

definition_component_validation.downstream_revision_policy: N21-N28 must treat drift bounds as test targets; changes require source-backed pass/fail evidence, not semantic preference
definition_component_validation.risk_if_omitted: any change could be relabeled as same basin or no change could be allowed
source_basis: N16 boundary-state maturity and drift controls
N18 limited h4/L5 horizon and bottleneck constraints

definition_component_validation.definition_role: minimum internal viability of the continuing basin
definition_component_validation.downstream_revision_policy: N21-N28 must instantiate numeric floors per primitive and record floor-crossing controls
definition_component_validation.risk_if_omitted: a basin could count as continuing after losing its support/coherence basis

definition_component_validation.downstream_revision_policy: N21-N28 may refine boundary criteria only by preserving N16/N19 separability and unsafe-claim blockers
definition_component_validation.risk_if_omitted: merge, leakage, or external structure could be misread as continuation
source_basis: N16 AP6 artifact-level self/environment boundary classification

definition_component_validation.definition_role: prevents flow/leakage/extraction from masquerading as persistence
definition_component_validation.downstream_revision_policy: N21-N28 must make flux bounds operational in their own geometry and fail closed when leakage/merge/extraction explains success
definition_component_validation.risk_if_omitted: support transfer, leakage, merge pressure, or extraction could look like same-basin persistence
source_basis: N16 directional-flux challenge and boundary-state sweep
N17 resource/support and shared-medium loop controls
N18 shared-medium and cross-axis bottleneck stress

definition_component_validation.downstream_revision_policy: N21-N28 must include replay/digest inputs sufficient to reconstruct the claimed primitive row
definition_component_validation.risk_if_omitted: hidden runtime state or post-hoc construction could define continuity
source_basis: N17 loop replay and order controls
N18 long-horizon replay and stale-axis controls

definition_component_validation.definition_role: named ways the row must fail closed
definition_component_validation.downstream_revision_policy: N21-N28 may add failure modes when source-backed tests expose new ways to fake continuation; they may not remove existing blockers without evidence
definition_component_validation.risk_if_omitted: later rows could pass by exploiting unnamed failure channels

definition_component_validation.definition_role: unsafe semantic and native-support claim boundary
definition_component_validation.downstream_revision_policy: Blocked relabels are non-converting labels; they cannot become proxy metrics or substrate-carried evidence in N21-N28
definition_component_validation.risk_if_omitted: agency, choice, goal, selfhood, native support, or sentience labels could become evidence

definition_component_validation.definition_role: shared fail-closed control surface for every primitive
definition_component_validation.downstream_revision_policy: N21-N28 can add primitive-specific controls but cannot remove the minimum shared template
definition_component_validation.risk_if_omitted: later primitive evidence could pass through label-only, proxy-only, hidden support, or native-support relabel paths

definition_correctness_boundary: I5 definitions are correctness constraints for future primitive tests. They are necessary contract gates derived from prior artifacts and method sources, not direct proof that any primitive is supported.
definition_revision_policy: N21-N28 cannot redefine basin signature, continuation condition, proxy-only success, or producer-residue classification ad hoc in order to pass. A later experiment may only revise an I5 definition by recording source-backed evidence that the definition is over- or under-constraining, while preserving N19 AP4/AP5 gap propagation and unsafe claim blockers.

medium_debt.claim_boundary: medium debt is a placeholder for later graph-substrate and agentic-ecology work, not ant-ecology implementation in N20

purpose: Define same-basin continuation criteria, replay requirements, and fail-closed controls for all N20 becoming-primitive contract rows.
